title: UNOPTFLAT reports should mention isolate_assignments
body:
Apparent combinational loops can arise from variables that need to be split, but also from overly large always blocks with many assignments. The former is solved with `split_var` and the latter with `isolate_assignments`, but the warnings that come up with UNOPTFLAT only mention the former.

I've written a simple patch for the warning to mention `isolate_assignments` (#5942), but might it be preferrable to incorporate some logic for detecting loops which might be broken by it?
